NE1000: The Structured Stretch Cotton Cap is our best-selling New Era, full stop — roughly 2,500 embroidered across nearly a hundred orders. It's the fitted-feel everyday cap: stretch band, structured front, big-league finish. Company logos land sharp on the front panel, and crews wear it enough that the reorder usually beats the season.
NE400: The Flat Bill Snapback is the New Era for brands with an edge — nearly 900 decorated here across 95 orders. The flat brim and structured crown carry a bold front embroidery or a stitched patch, and the snapback fits the whole crew from one box. Breweries, gyms, and trades that skew young keep this one moving.
NE200: The Adjustable Structured Cap holds the most telling stat in our New Era program: almost 200 separate orders. It's the classic company cap — structured front, adjustable back, no learning curve — and teams reorder it year after year with the same embroidered logo. If you want the safe New Era, this is it.
NE205: The Snapback Trucker brings New Era's build quality to the mesh-back silhouette everyone asks for — nearly 900 have left our shop with logos and patches on the foam front. It's the step-up trucker: same relaxed look as the budget caps, noticeably better structure and finish.
NE1020: The Stretch Mesh Cap is the fitted-feel performance New Era — stretch band comfort with a breathable mesh back, in the brand's clean athletic shape. Over 450 decorated here, and it carries one of the best margins in our headwear wall. Front-panel embroidery reads crisp on the smooth face.
NE4030: The Standard Fit Snapback Trucker is New Era's current take on the everyday trucker — a middle crown height that suits more heads than the tall classic.450-plus have gone out with front embroidery and patches; it's quickly becoming the default trucker in our New Era lineup.
NE204: The Snapback Contrast Front Mesh Cap two-tones the trucker formula — solid front panel, contrasting mesh, ready-made for a logo that pops.270 decorated across 22 orders; teams pick it when the plain trucker feels too quiet.
NE403: The Original Fit Snapback Trucker is the taller, classic-crown version of New Era's trucker — the old-school profile that's come all the way back around. The high foam front is prime patch and embroidery territory; 240-plus have shipped with logos on them.
NE1121: The Diamond Era Stretch Cap runs New Era's performance fabric through their fitted-feel silhouette — light, wicking, and stretchy enough to cover a crew in two sizes. It's the cap for teams that sweat in theirs; embroidery holds up wash after wash.
NE407: The Camo Flat Bill Snapback puts a camouflage crown under New Era's flat brim — 130-plus decorated for crews and hunting-adjacent brands. A solid-color front panel keeps the logo legible over the pattern; patches work even better.
NE404: The Original Fit Diamond Era Flat Bill pairs New Era's performance fabric with the street-style flat brim — a snapback that dries fast and keeps its shape. Teams that want the flat-bill look with athletic function land here.
NE1090: The Tech Mesh Cap is the lightest-wearing New Era we stock — full performance mesh in a structured stretch fit. It carries a 70-percent-plus margin in our program and embroiders cleaner than a mesh cap has any right to. Summer crews and running clubs are the regulars.
NE406: The Perforated Performance Cap vents through laser perforations instead of mesh panels — a sleeker athletic look that still breathes. Ninety-plus decorated here; it suits tone-on-tone embroidery for a modern finish.
NE1091: The Tonal Camo Stretch Tech Mesh Cap layers a subtle same-color camo into New Era's lightest performance cap — pattern without the loudness. A small embroidered mark keeps the whole thing understated and premium.
NE4020: The Standard Fit Flat Bill Snapback carries the flat-brim look in New Era's middle crown height — flatter learning curve than the original tall fit, same street credibility. It runs one of the highest margins on our cap wall and takes patches beautifully.
NE212: The A-Frame Snapback Mesh Back is New Era's five-panel trucker — the A-frame front gives a taller, flatter face that carries a big patch or bold embroidery. Sixty went out in its first orders here; expect that number to climb.
NE208: The Recycled Snapback is New Era's sustainability play — recycled-content fabric in the classic snapback silhouette. Companies with environmental commitments pick it so their headwear matches their messaging. Decorates identically to the standard caps.
NE209: The Performance Dash Adjustable Cap is New Era's lightweight runner — wicking fabric, unstructured comfort, an adjustable back. It's the athletic dad-cap of the line and a natural for race events and training staffs.
NE1120: The Stretch Mesh Contrast Stitch Cap adds visible contrast stitching to New Era's stretch-mesh formula — a detail that reads custom before the logo even goes on. Fitted-feel comfort in two sizes covers the whole roster.
NE207: The Snapback Low Profile Trucker sits closer to the head than the classic trucker — the relaxed, broken-in look with New Era's build quality. A low-key embroidered mark suits its profile; patches work too.
NE402: The Original Fit Flat Bill Snapback is the purist's New Era — the full-height crown and dead-flat brim that started the whole look. The big front panel is the best patch real estate in the line.
NE302: The Youth Stretch Mesh Cap scales New Era's fitted-feel performance cap down to kid sizes — teams order it so the youth squad matches the varsity look exactly. Same embroidery, smaller crown.
NE501: The 9SEVENTY Perforated Snapback runs New Era's numbered-silhouette pedigree through a vented performance build — a sleek snapback that breathes. A tight tone-on-tone logo finishes it like retail.
NE800: The Hex Era Bucket Hat is New Era's performance bucket — hex-textured wicking fabric in the full-brim shape that's back in force. Events, golf tournaments, and sun-heavy crews order it; embroidery goes front and center.
NE214: The Pivot Stretch Band Snapback blends the fitted feel of a stretch band with snapback adjustability — New Era engineering applied to the fit problem every crew order has. Clean panels, sharp embroidery.
NE409: The Pivot Flat Bill Snapback carries the Pivot line's hybrid stretch-plus-snap fit under a flat brim — the street silhouette that actually fits everyone who grabs one from the box.
NE410: The Foam Rope Trucker revives the full retro package — foam front, mesh back, rope across the brim. It's the throwback cap of the New Era line and a patch's natural habitat.
NE701: The Ballistic Cap is New Era's tough-wear model — durable ballistic fabric built for job sites rather than sidelines. A workwear-grade New Era for crews that destroy lesser caps.
NE704: The Shadow Stretch Heather Colorblock Cap mixes heathered texture with colorblocked panels — a designed look that photographs like team retail. A small clean logo keeps it balanced.
NE211: The A-Frame Snapback is the five-panel silhouette in solid fabric — a tall, flat front face made for patches and big embroidery, without the mesh back. Street-leaning brands pick it.
NE215: The 9TWENTY Unstructured Mesh Snapback is New Era's relaxed dad-cap silhouette with a mesh back — soft crown, curved bill, easy wear. A modest front logo suits it perfectly.
NE219: The Performance Dash Adjustable Visor is the open-top option in New Era's athletic line — wicking sweatband, adjustable fit. Golf outings, tennis programs, and summer events order it alongside the matching caps.
NE304: The Youth Original Fit Diamond Era Flat Bill brings the performance flat-bill down to youth sizes — team programs order it so the kids' caps match the club look stitch for stitch.
NE1130: The 39THIRTY Performance Stretch Mesh Cap is New Era's famous stretch-fitted silhouette in breathable performance trim — the closest thing to the on-field feel in our lineup. Two sizes cover the roster; embroidery lands sharp on the structured front.
NE907: The Recycled Cuff Beanie is our best-selling New Era knit — nearly 300 decorated, most in big single batches. Recycled yarn, classic cuff, and a fold that doubles the embroidery canvas. Winter crew orders and holiday gifts keep it moving.
NE900: The Knit Beanie is New Era's straightforward winter staple — a clean cuffed knit that takes a tight embroidered logo on the fold. Two hundred-plus through our machines; it ships out every November by the box.
NE902: The Sideline Beanie is the one you see on broadcast — the sport-styled knit with New Era's game-day pedigree. Teams and fan-gear programs order it when the beanie itself needs to say athletics.
NE905: The Speckled Beanie flecks contrasting yarn through the knit — texture that reads retail rather than uniform. A small cuff logo finishes it; twenty went out in its first order here.
NE908: The Core Classic Cuff Beanie is New Era's essential knit — the clean, everyday cuffed silhouette at the value end of the line. Embroiders sharp on the fold and covers a whole crew affordably.
NE911: The Faux Fur Pom Beanie tops New Era's knit with a statement pom — the winter-market look for boutiques, resorts, and holiday gift orders. Cuff embroidery keeps the branding tidy under the fun.
NE903: The Ribbed Tailgate Beanie brings a chunky ribbed texture to New Era's knit line — the parking-lot classic. The wide cuff carries an embroidered logo cleanly.
NE904: The Colorblock Cuffed Beanie splits crown and cuff into contrasting colors — a built-in two-tone that makes even a simple logo look designed. Team programs match it to their palette.
NE906: The On-Field Knit Beanie is styled after the sideline gear the pros actually wear — New Era's sport knit with the athletic details. Fan programs and team stores are its natural home.
NE909: The Knit Chilled Pom Beanie is the pom-topped version of New Era's everyday knit — winter-market fun with big-league build quality. Cuff embroidery, holiday gift orders, done.
NE910: The Marled Knit Pom Beanie twists two yarn colors through the whole knit — a heathered, boutique-y look under the pom. It's the beanie for brands that want their winter gear to look bought, not issued.
